All right, now that you know the structure of your email, let's talk about what we're going to write in these emails. Now if you remember at the very beginning, we talked about how all of our email subscribers, they're living on this island. So we're thinking about all of our email subscribers on Indecision Island and then we are over here on Results Island. Now only you know the full extent of the results that you can create for your customers. Only you truly know the transformation that can happen. And so what we've got to do with our email is start to get all these things out of your brain into words that all your subscribers on Indecision Island can start to understand. So we're going to have four different types of emails that we'll be sending. And the point of all these emails is helping all your subscribers start to take steps closer and closer to your results island. Every single time we send one of these types of emails, we know in our hands, in our hearts, in our minds why we're sending this email. It's not just to say, hey, don't forget about me or, hey, did you see the sale that I'm having this weekend or, hey, did you sign up yet? No, we have so much more intention behind our emails because we are bringing our subscribers on a journey. So I want you to think about all your subscribers over here. They're so far away from results island, right? And so the very first type of messaging we want to send them with our emails is for the people that are really far away. They can hardly hear us. They're not at all interested yet in buying from us. So for those people, all those people on that island that are far, far away from making a decision, we want to send emails that are going to entertain. And do you know the best, the most entertaining of all emails? Those are emails that tell stories. So the majority of the emails that you're going to write are going to fall into the entertaining category. Why? Because the majority of the people on your email list, they're in the far away category.

## [03:01 - 06:01]

They're not buyers. They're not even yet thinking about buying. And so we want to get those people opening your emails, reading your emails and getting to know you and getting to like you more and more each and every time you send them a message. But for now, just know that one of your main jobs in writing emails is simply telling stories. How fun is that? Now for those that have read your stories and started to decide, you know, I like her. I trust him. That was a pretty cool story. He just told me. For those people, we're now going to send the next stage of the journey message. And that is informational emails. These are educational emails. These are teaching emails. So in these emails, we've already gathered their like. We've already started to get them to trust us. And now we're going to bring it one step further. We're going to say, hey, come a little closer. Because I got more than just a story. I actually have a step by step. I have a how to. I have a way that you can take the story you just heard and make it a reality for yourself. Now don't worry, we're not getting into all the details here in our emails. We're giving micro wins. That is the term you want to remember when you're thinking about these emails. Little tiny successes, little micro wins in these emails. That's what you're giving in your informational emails. When they say that, they are ready to go to the next step. And that's what we do in our third type of email, our third part of the journey. And this is what I call showcase. So a showcase email is where you finally have permission to show off. You can talk about how incredible you are. You can talk about your results. You can share before and afters. You can do behind the scenes. You can do the transformation and show what you were and what you are now. And you can do this in the micro. You can do what you were yesterday and what you are today. Showing is a time not just to show off your own results, but to show off the results of your students and your clients. This is when you get to start to help your reader see, this doesn't just work for her. This isn't just good for him. I mean, look at these people that are just like me. They got results too. The showcase emails is where we start to really bring it all together for our readers. Where they see, oh, I like this person. I understand.

## [06:01 - 09:06]

I'm starting to get this. This is starting to make sense. And then, whoa, now I see how this works. Not just for one person, but for a lot of people. So this is the showcase email. This is the third part of the journey. And this is when we are getting these people so close to our results island because they like us. They trust us. They understand us. And now, once they see what we can do, they know they need us. They know that without us, they're not going to have those same results. And that moves us to the fourth type of email. And that is the invitational email. That is the invite email. And this is where we offer them an opportunity to do something new. I don't know about you, but I love doing something new. I love going somewhere new. I love a new opportunity. I love a new trip. I love new. And so do your readers. Everyone is scrolling every single day looking for something new, looking for something different, looking for something to get them out of the monotony of the boredom of the numbing that they feel in their everyday life. And this is what we get to do in this fourth type of email. We got to say, Hey, you're invited. Hey, you can come over here. You can start something new right now. And I have the opportunity for you that is perfect. So invitational emails are going to be a very small part of the emails that we send, but they are the most important in terms of moving our business along. Because this is when we say, Hey, do you want it or do you not? Are you ready or no? This is where we create opportunity for them to go to the next step with us and not just to stay in indecision, but to finally move over to results Island. This is where we start to build the want. They've started to liken trust us with our stories when we entertain them. They understood us when we inform and teach them. So those are the four stages. Those are the four types of emails. So no longer will you sit down and look at your computer and go, I don't know what to write. I need to write an email, but I don't know what to say. Instead, you're going to categorize your email. You're going to say, Okay, what stage of the journey are my email subscribers on? Which stage of people do I want to talk to with this email? And then you're simply going to categorize. Is this an entertaining email and informing email, a showcasing email or an inviting email?
